back_translation: Adam slept with his wife Eve. She conceived and gave birth to Cain. She said, "I have obtained a son with the help of the LORD."
Later she gave birth to Abel, Cain's brother. Abel was a shepherd, while Cain was a farmer.
After some time, Cain brought produce from the ground as an offering to the LORD.
Abel also brought firstborn animals from his flock, including their fat. The LORD looked with favor on Abel and his offering,
but He did not look with favor on Cain and his offering. So Cain became very angry, and his face fell.
The LORD said to Cain, "Why are you angry, and why has your face fallen?
If you do what is good, will you not be accepted? But if you do not do what is good, sin is crouching at the door. It desires you, but you must conquer it."
Cain spoke to his brother Abel, [saying, "Let us go out to the field."] When they were in the field, Cain rose up against his brother Abel and killed him.
The LORD said to Cain, "Where is your brother Abel?" Cain replied, "I do not know. Am I my brother's keeper?"
He said, "What have you done? The voice of your brother's blood is crying out to Me from the ground.
Now you are cursed away from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother's blood from your hand.
When you work the ground, it will no longer yield its produce to you. You will be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth."
Cain said to the LORD, "My punishment is too heavy to bear.
Behold, today You have driven me from the land, and I will have to hide from Your face. I will be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and whoever finds me will kill me."
The LORD said to him, "If anyone kills Cain, that person will be avenged seven times over." The LORD placed a mark on Cain so that no one who found him would harm him.
Cain went out from the presence of the LORD and settled in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
Cain slept with his wife. She conceived and gave birth to Enoch. Cain built a city and named it after his son Enoch.
Enoch was the father of Irad. Irad was the father of Mehujael. Mehujael was the father of Methusael. Methusael was the father of Lamech.
Lamech took two wives, one named Adah and the other named Zillah.
Adah gave birth to Jabal. He was the ancestor of those who live in tents and raise livestock.
His brother's name was Jubal. He was the ancestor of all who play the harp and flute.
Zillah also gave birth to Tubal-cain, who was a forger of all kinds of bronze and iron tools. The sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.
Lamech said to his two wives, "Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; wives of Lamech, listen to my speech. For I have killed a man for wounding me, and a young man for bruising me.
If Cain is to be avenged sevenfold, then Lamech will be avenged seventy-sevenfold."
Adam slept with his wife again. She gave birth to a son and named him Seth, saying, "God has granted me another offspring in place of Abel, since Cain killed him."
Seth also had a son, and he named him Enosh. At that time, people began to call upon the name of the LORD.
